mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-test p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in 65 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below zero in post-recession windows.

mechanism_narrative: Escalating inflation combined with restrictive policy forces central banks to maintain higher rates, increasing equity risk premiums and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when inflation accelerates alongside rate hikes, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll additions signal robust economic activity that reduces default risk and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surges coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in 60 percent of cases, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2016.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial output and rising retail sales indicates inventory depletion and supply chain bottlenecks that compress margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns, or forward returns remain positive when supply constraints emerge.
